one_line: The research case-resolution workspace that converts a patient's molecular and clinical context into a traceable map of biology, vulnerabilities, mechanism-aligned options, trial exploration, evidence, missing data, and caveats — the human decision surface of CrisPRO, RUO.
status_of_document: PRODUCT TRUTH CONTRACT (RUO). Structured knowledge artifact grounding every public claim to a real KB entity (Qdrant collection crispro_kb_v3 + CrisPRO Neo4j graph). Not a page, not marketing copy, not a deck.

framing.is: Tumor Board is the research case-resolution workspace (the CrisPRO 'Oncology Arbiter') that converts a patient's molecular and clinical context into a traceable map of biology, vulnerabilities, mechanism-aligned options, trial exploration, evidence, missing data, and caveats. It is the human decision surface of the platform.
framing.is_not_a_separate_biology_engine: Tumor Board is NOT a separate biology engine. It is a surface over the SAME shared CrisPRO intelligence layer used by Interception and In-Silico Trials; it retrieves and presents that intelligence, it does not re-derive biology independently.
framing.is_not_four_demo_patients: Tumor Board is NOT 'four demo patients'. The demo_sample entities (case_full, nsclc, ak_mbd4_lof_case, biopsy, screening, elo_rank) are explicitly 'demo sample for regulatory-posture-locked arbiter walkthrough' fixtures that illustrate the pipeline; they are not the product and must not be presented as its evidence base.
framing.regulatory: Research Use Only. Not FDA-cleared, not CE-marked, not intended for clinical use. RUO posture applies to EVERY arbiter output. Must remain RUO unless and until validated for clinical deployment.

evidence.note: Explicit RUO posture on every arbiter output

decision.text: For a specific patient case, which biology, vulnerabilities, mechanism-aligned options, and trials warrant investigation by the treating/translational team — and what is still unknown — assembled into one interpretable, traceable output that a human uses to decide next steps.

evidence.note: Structured verdict + evidence pointers + RUO disclaimer

problem.text: A clinician or translational team has a molecular profile but must manually assemble the relevant biology, options, trial context, risks, missing information, and evidence boundaries. This is slow, inconsistent, and often loses the evidence trail — and generic AI tools deflect into safety refusals instead of surfacing what the data supports.

evidence.note: Model-card + knowledge retrieval step that assembles evidence

input.text: A patient's available clinical and molecular context (e.g. pathology report, molecular profile, optionally imaging). Input is normalized and contract-enforced before processing.

workflow_plain_language.text: CrisPRO's Oncology Arbiter runs a five-layer pipeline: L1 Gateway (auth/entry) → L2 Input Normalizer (report parsing) → L3 Evidence Retriever (knowledge + model-card retrieval) → L4 Co-scientist (L4a generate hypotheses, L4b reflect/critique, L4c Elo-rank) → L5 structured Arbiter Output with evidence pointers and an RUO disclaimer. It organizes what the profile supports; it does not autonomously treat.

evidence.note: Co-scientist generation/reflection/ranking is the reasoning core

output.text: A traceable research case package: molecular context, mechanism hypotheses, synthetic-lethality/vulnerability findings, therapy/trial exploration, an evidence trace, safety/governance flags, and an explicit missing-data and confidence display — each item pointing back to its evidence.

proof_and_limits.text: The pipeline is real and runnable; several inference components are deterministic heuristics or proxy models (e.g. monai-detector-heuristic, nsclc_lung_heuristic_v0, therapy-nccn-lite), explicitly RESEARCH USE ONLY and NOT diagnostic. Demo cases are walkthrough fixtures. Tumor Board is NOT clinical decision support and does NOT predict individual patient outcomes.

evidence.note: Deterministic rules-based lookup, not an ML model

engagement.text: A first engagement is a research case walkthrough on de-identified or synthetic cases: the team provides molecular/clinical context; CrisPRO returns the traceable case package for research review. Any clinical use requires prior validation and is out of current scope.

shared_intelligence_cross_map.description: The SAME shared intelligence layer feeds all three product surfaces. Rows = shared intelligence; columns = how each surface uses it.

output_section_inventory.note: Inventory of every Tumor Board (Oncology Arbiter) output section / component, classified honestly by execution status. Heuristic/proxy models are 'executable_and_live' as pipeline components but are RUO proxies, NOT validated clinical inference — flagged in caveat.

sections.section: L1 Gateway (API entry + auth + rate limiting)
L2 Input Normalizer (report parser + input contract)
sections.caveat: Regex/parse based; report_parser_clinicalbert_v1 is the ML upgrade path (fallback).
Real fine-tuned token-classification model; used as parser upgrade over regex.
Retrieves from KB/model cards; quality bounded by ingested corpus.
Hypothesis generation; research-grade, not validated clinical recommendation.
Elo ranking is a preference ordering, not an outcome probability.
Deterministic NCCN-lite rules lookup — NOT an ML model, NOT prescribing; RUO fallback.
Heuristic gradient/edge detector — RESEARCH USE ONLY, NOT a diagnostic detector.
Proxy heuristic — research/demonstration only, not for diagnosis.

sections.section: Multimodal reasoning models (MedGemma 27B / 4B, MedSigLIP-448, TxGemma, biopsy MedSigLIP probe)
sections.caveat: Model cards ingested; heavy multimodal weights are integration-dependent and not guaranteed live in every deployment.

sections.section: L5 Arbiter Output (structured verdict + evidence pointers + RUO disclaimer)
Synthetic-lethality / vulnerability findings surfaced into case
sections.caveat: SL evidence exists in KB (sl_evidence_record, sl_engine); patient-specific application is a hypothesis, not a validated therapeutic fact.
Draws on trial vector library / program dossiers; exploration only, not enrollment/responder prediction.
Optional single-page UI served under /ui/; RUO, not for diagnostic use; deployment-optional.

sections.section: Demo cases (case_full, nsclc, ak_mbd4_lof_case, biopsy, screening, elo_rank)
sections.caveat: Regulatory-posture-locked walkthrough fixtures — NOT the product's evidence base; must not be presented as 'four patients'.

sections.section: GBM ZEB1 escape annotation in case output
sections.caveat: GBM ZEB1 PERMANENTLY CLOSED (3 datasets null/contradicted) — must not appear as an escape marker in any case.

sections.section: LATIFY delta / DDR 0.983 as case figures
sections.caveat: QUARANTINED figures — must not be surfaced as case evidence.

capability_to_buyer_value.product_output: Parsed molecular/clinical context with evidence pointers

capability_to_buyer_value.customer_buys: Candidate dependency exploration for the case
capability_to_buyer_value.product_output: Candidate dependency list with evidence tier
Therapy/trial options to investigate with evidence trace

capability_to_buyer_value.customer_buys: Knowing what is supported and what is unknown
capability_to_buyer_value.product_output: Evidence pointers + explicit gaps and confidence
capability_to_buyer_value.must_not_claim: Completeness or certainty of the case assessment

capability_to_buyer_value.customer_buys: Automatic surfacing of claim boundaries in-case
capability_to_buyer_value.product_output: RUO flags, quarantines, and prohibited-claim guards
capability_to_buyer_value.must_not_claim: That flags substitute for clinical/regulatory review

prohibited_claims_global: Tumor Board is clinical decision support or provides a treatment directive.
CrisPRO / the Oncology Arbiter is FDA-cleared or CE-marked.
Tumor Board is 'four demo patients' or that the demo fixtures are its evidence base.
Tumor Board is a separate biology engine (it is a surface over the shared intelligence layer).
Heuristic/proxy models (monai-detector-heuristic, nsclc_lung_heuristic_v0, therapy-nccn-lite) perform validated clinical diagnosis or prescribing.
Synthetic-lethality findings are patient-specific therapeutic facts.
Trial exploration predicts responders, enrollment, or outcomes.
GBM ZEB1 is an escape marker in a case; LATIFY delta / DDR 0.983 are valid case figures (QUARANTINED).

_provenance.scope: v3_0_0 + kg_v2 ingested; every evidence receipt verified present in Qdrant collection crispro_kb_v3.
